top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Zapopan's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
GFNY Zapopan's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
GFNY Zapopan's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 91 miles.
At mile 60 of 91, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Zapopan. Train it until it feels boring.
At GFNY Zapopan, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 91 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Zapopan, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 91 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,049ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Zapopan’s 91 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Zapopan. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Zapopan requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 91 miles of mixed terrain in Zapopan (Guadalajara), Jalisco, Mexico.
